title: Luggage case with axle-less wheels
abstract: An axle-free dust labyrinth wheel includes an internal rim within an external rim that rotate relative to each other via rollers disposed within a ball cage. A ground interface member is circumferentially disposed on the external rim and defines an extension portion that extends toward the internal rim. A side cover is connected to one of the internal and external rims and covers a portion of a first side of the axle-free wheel opposite from a second side and the extension portion of the ground interface member. A dust labyrinth having a section on the first side of the axle-free wheel is defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the side cover. A section on the second side of the axle-free wheel is defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the extension portion of the ground interface member.
claims:
Claims An axle-free dust labyrinth wheel comprising: an internal rim within an external rim between which there are rollers disposed within a ball cage to permit the internal and external rims to rotate relative to each other; a ground interface member circumferentially disposed on the external rim, the ground interface member defining an extension portion that extends toward the internal rim; a side cover connected to one of the internal and external rims and covering a portion of a first side of the axle-free wheel and opposite from a second side of the axle-free wheel and the extension portion of the ground interface member; a sealable dust labyrinth having a section on the first side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim. the external rim, and the side cover, and a section on the second side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the extension portion of the ground interface member. The axle-free dust labyrinth wheel according to claim 1 , wherein the side cover is configured to connect to the internal rim with a resilient snap fixable on a surface of the internal rim. An axle-free dust labyrinth wheel comprising: an internal rim within an external rim between which there are rollers disposed within a ball cage to permit the internal and external rims to rotate relative to each other; a ground interface member circumferentially disposed on the external rim, the external rim defining an extension portion that extends toward the internal rim; a side cover connected to one of the internal and external rims and covering a portion of a first side of the axle-free wheel and opposite from a second side of the axle-free wheel and the extension portion of the external rim; a sealable dust labyrinth having a section on the first side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the side 

 cover and a section on the second side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the extension portion of the external rim. The axle-free dust labyrinth wheel according to claim 3, wherein the side cover is configured to connect to the internal rim with a resilient snap fixable on a surface of the internal rim. A luggage case comprising: a storage chamber formed in the luggage case; a cover configured to provide access to the storage chamber; at least two axle-free wheels rotatably connected to the luggage case that permit the luggage case to be rolled along a ground surface; each of the axle-free wheels including an internal rim within an external rim between which there are rollers disposed within a ball cage to permit the internal and external rims to rotate relative to each other; a ground interface member circumferentially disposed on the external rim; an extension portion that extends toward the internal rim and extending from one of the ground interface member and the external rim; a side cover connected to one of the internal and external rims and covering a portion of a first side of the axle-free wheel and opposite from a second side of the axle-free wheel and the extension portion; a dust labyrinth having a section on the first side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the side cover and a section on the second side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the extension portion. The luggage case according to claim 5, wherein the side cover is configured to connect to the internal rim with a resilient snap fixable on a surface of the internal rim. 7. The luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising side walls of the luggage case that each form a recess in which a portion of one of the at least two axle-free wheels is disposed in proximity to the storage chamber, wherein a portion of the recess is configured to cover a portion the ground interface member associated with one of the at least two axle- free wheels. 8. The luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising side walls of the luggage case that each form a recess in which a portion of one of the at least two axle-free wheels is disposed, wherein a portion of one of the at least two axle-free wheels extends beyond the recess and the side walls a distance to establish a clearance distance between a bearing area on which the at least two axle-free wheels are configured to contact and a portion of the storage chamber closest to the bearing area. 9. The luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising: a telescoping handle that extends from a top wall of the luggage case and that permits the luggage case to be moved between a tow condition and an upright condition; a bottom wall of the luggage case opposite the top wall that defines an arcuate shape disposed between each of the recesses formed in the side walls of the luggage case, wherein the arcuate shape is proximate the storage chamber and defines a distance from the bottom wall of the luggage case and the bearing area, and wherein the distance from the bottom wall of the luggage case and the bearing area increases when the luggage case is moved from the upright condition to the tow condition. 10. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5, wherein the recess includes a surface establishing an angle with a side wall of the luggage case whose value in a first and third portion of the recess is less than a value of the angle in the second portion of the recess that is disposed between the first portion and the third portion. 1 1. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 10, wherein the value of the angle in a portion of the second portion of the recess is about ninety degrees. 12. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 10, wherein the value of the angle in a portion of the first portion of the recess and a portion of the third portion of the recess is less than 45 degrees. 13. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 10, wherein the value of the angle in a portion of the first portion of the recess and a portion of the third portion of the recess decreases in value the further its position is from the second portion of the recess. 14. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 10, wherein the value of the angle in a portion of the first portion of the recess and a portion of the third portion of the recess gradually changes from a perpendicular angle proximate the second portion of the recess to a value of the angle being less than 45 degrees at a position in the portion of the first portion of the recess and the portion of the third portion of the recess farthest from the second portion. 15. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising a flexible strap configured for towing the wheeled luggage case in a group manner. 16. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 15, wherein the flexible strap is deployable from an inner cavity defined within a portion of the storage chamber and through a port at a top wall of the luggage case and retractable by one of a manually movable slider and an elastic member connected to the flexible strap. 17. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising an interchangeable and selectively deployable supporting member mounted opposite to the labyrinth axle-free wheels, wherein the luggage case sits the at least two axle-free wheels and the interchangeable and selectively deployable supporting member when in a steady vertical position. 18. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising at least two spaced apart rollers rotatable around an axis perpendicular to a rotation axis of the labyrinth axle-free wheels, wherein the luggage case sits the at least two axle-free wheels and the least two spaced apart rollers when in a steady vertical position. 19. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising at least one light source providing internal illumination within the storage chamber. 20. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising at least one light source and an interchangeable and selectively deployable supporting member mounted opposite to the labyrinth axle-free wheels, wherein the luggage case sits the at least two axle-free wheels and the interchangeable and selectively deployable supporting member when in a steady vertical position, wherein the light source directs illumination outward from the supporting member. 21. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising at least one light source that directs illumination outward from one of the at least two axle-free wheels. 22. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 21 , wherein the at least one light source that directs illumination outward from one of the at least two axle-free wheels is a plurality of circumferentially distributed light sources. 23. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 5 further comprising a wireless pad formed in a wall of the luggage case for wirelessly charging mobile devices. 24. A wheeled luggage case for towing by a user, the wheeled luggage case comprising: a rigid shell including at least two members having a hinged connection movable between an open condition that allows access to a storage compartment in the wheeled luggage case and a closed condition that prevents entry into the storage compartment, the rigid shell having an envelope dimension in the closed condition; a quick-release fastener configured for releasably fastening the two members to each other; a telescopic handle for towing the wheeled luggage case by the user; the telescopic handle extendable from the rigid shell by up to twice the envelope dimension; two axle-free wheels rotatably connected to the luggage case that permit the luggage case to be towed by the user, each of the axle-free wheels includes an internal rim within an external rim between which there are rollers disposed within a ball cage to permit the internal and external rims to rotate relative to each other; a ground interface member circumferentially disposed on the external rim of each of the two axle-free wheels and defining an outer diameter of the two axle-free wheels that are greater than or about equal to one-third of the envelope dimension. 25. The wheeled luggage case of claim 24 further comprising: an extension portion that extends toward the internal rim and extending from one of the ground interface member and the external rim; a side cover connected to one of the internal and external rims and covering a portion of a first side of the axle-free wheel and opposite from a second side of the axle-free wheel and the extension portion; and a dust labyrinth having a section on the first side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the side cover and a section on the second side of the axle-free wheel defined by cooperation between portions of the internal rim, the external rim, and the extension portion. 26. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 25, wherein the two axle-free wheels each include at least one friction-type bearing. 27. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 26, wherein the friction type bearing is one of a skid bearing and an oil bearing. 28. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 24 further comprising at least one partition member dividing the case into at least two compartments; and strap members that hold the at least one partition member between the two members of the rigid shell 29. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 28, wherein the partition member is removable and exchangeable. 30. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 28, wherein a position of the partition member is adjustable relative to the strap members and the two members of the rigid case. 31. The wheeled luggage case according to claim 24, wherein the ground interface member is a tire.
